LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF LABORER'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD - PETITION IS UNOPPOSED BY, AND IS GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO, RESPONDENTS DAVID ROSSI, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS TUCKER ENGINEERING, AND ATTORNEYS' FEES ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PETITIONER AND AGAINST RESPONDENTS DAVID ROSSI, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS TUCKER ENGINEERING. THE PETITION IS DENIED AS TO RESPONDENT SUNSTONE CONSTRUCTION INC {SCI}. (SEE MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award; Index Of Federal Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CONCHITA LOZANO-BATISTA OF WEINBERG, ROGER & ROSENFELD, TEL. NO. (510) 337-1001 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF LABORER'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award - Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award is unopposed by Respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering. The Petition is granted with respect to Respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering, and attorneys' fees are awarded in favor of Petitioner and against respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering. The Petition is denied as to Respondent Sunstone Construction Inc {SCI}. SCI did not sign an agreement containing arbitration clause. Section 11 of the Master Agreement, cited in the Petition 7 filed December 15, 2011 does state that its terms apply to subcontractors but SCI is not a party to the Master Agreement. SCI notified Petitioner of this reasoning on the filing of the Petition, and SCI has unjustifiably incurred fees in the sum of $3,533 in responding to Petitioner's frivolous filings. However SCI has not provided the Court with authority either under federal law (the parties appear to agree federal law may apply) or state law (i.e. CCP 128.7) authorizing the award of fees in favor of a respondent who successfully resists a petition to confirm. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Not reported. =302/ck
